global_context.scene_description: A bold editorial cartoon-illustrated split-frame composition, single image with a clean vertical divider down the middle. Same homeowner character drawn in both frames — recognizable, cartoon proportions, expressive. Editorial cartoon illustration style with theatrical color blocking, NOT photorealistic, NOT flat infographic. LEFT FRAME (cool blue-grey background, anxious mood): the homeowner stands hunched over slightly, a comically tall WOBBLY STACK of dread-objects piled precariously on his head. The stack from bottom to top: a giant bold red '$20,000' invoice paper, on top of that a calendar marked '2 WEEKS' with red X marks, on top of that a sleazy salesman cartoon caricature in a cheap suit holding a clipboard, on top of that a stack of bills/paperwork, on top of that a chunk of demolition debris, on top a tiny stress lightning bolt squiggle. The stack leans precariously, sweat drops fly off the homeowner's head, his eyes are bugged, eyebrows furrowed, hands raised to try to balance the stack. RIGHT FRAME (warm sunlit background, calm relieved mood): the SAME homeowner stands upright and relaxed, NOTHING on his head, holding ONLY a single folded clean ASH quote/document at his side. Eyebrows raised in pleasant surprise, small content smile, posture loose, weight evenly distributed. Above the entire image, a clean banner-style headline reads in bold uppercase: 'WHAT YOU'VE BEEN CARRYING. WHAT IT ACTUALLY IS.' At the bottom, a small italic caption: 'New AC install — drop the stack.' A bold black vertical line separates the two halves. Mood: the visible relief of dropping a stack of imagined fears that were never real. Editorial cartoon illustration, theatrical color blocking, expressive character work.

lighting.quality: left frame cool/anxious, right frame warm/relieved

composition.framing: vertical split-frame, two equal halves divided by a bold vertical line, headline banner across top, small caption strip across bottom
composition.focal_point: the comically tall wobbly worry-stack on the left vs the empty-headed relief on the right
